name: Fortis Bank
description_short: Fortis Bank is a commercial banking partner offering business loans, treasury management, and deposit accounts to small and mid-market companies across multiple industries.
description_long: Fortis Bank positions itself as a dedicated commercial banking partner focused on serving business clients rather than individual consumers. The bank emphasizes long-term relationships and personalized service through hand-selected banking teams. Based on their website, Fortis Bank operates as a full-service commercial bank with physical locations and online banking capabilities. The company offers a comprehensive suite of commercial banking products including short and long-term commercial loans, lines of credit, deposit accounts (checking, savings, money market), secure online banking platforms, remote deposit capture, and treasury management solutions. Their lending expertise spans commercial real estate financing, term loans, working capital financing, partner buy-ins, and competitor acquisition financing. They also serve specialized sectors including law firm banking, private equity transactions, healthcare, contractors, and multifamily real estate. Fortis Bank differentiates itself through industry-specific expertise in sectors like commercial real estate, retail and hospitality, medical office facilities, and healthcare. They emphasize dedicated banking relationships, strategic advice, and innovative technology combined with experienced banking teams. Their marketing focuses on trustworthiness, secure tools, and understanding unique business needs. As a regional commercial bank, Fortis Bank is designed for established small to mid-market businesses rather than startups or individual consumers. The current categorization as "business-loans" is incomplete—this is a full-service bank offering multiple products beyond lending. The online banking interface and emphasis on deposit accounts place them more accurately in the broader "banking" category. Minimum loan amounts and specific APR ranges are not disclosed on their website.
pros: Industry-specific expertise across nine sectors including commercial real estate, healthcare, law firm banking, and private equity
Multiple lending products available including term loans, lines of credit, and commercial real estate financing
Dedicated banker relationships with hand-selected team members committed to understanding unique business needs
Secure online banking and mobile app access for 24/7 account management and cash flow oversight
Treasury management solutions for effective cash flow management
Remote deposit capture capability for convenience
Multiple physical locations available for in-person banking
cons: No specific loan amounts, APR ranges, or terms disclosed on website—borrowers must contact directly for pricing
Commercial focus means this bank is not suitable for personal banking or consumer loans
No information provided about minimum account balances, fees, or service charges
Website does not specify turnaround times for loan decisions or funding
Limited transparency on lending criteria or qualification requirements
best_for: Established small to mid-market businesses seeking comprehensive commercial banking relationships
Companies in specialized industries (law firms, healthcare, construction, private equity) needing sector-specific expertise
Businesses requiring multiple products including loans, deposit accounts, and treasury management under one banking partner
diagnosis: Fortis Bank is best for established small to mid-market businesses needing a comprehensive banking partner with industry-specific expertise and personalized service. The main caveat is that this is a commercial bank requiring business banking relationships; individual consumers should not apply, and specific loan terms and minimum thresholds are not publicly disclosed online.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Fortis Bank offers business loans, treasury management, and deposit accounts to small and mid-market companies.
